_about: Ground-truth labels for the Strata software-dev fixture bundle (fixtures/software-bundle/*). The live eval (Task 20) ingests these docs through the real pipeline and scores the resulting graph + CQ answers against this file. Entity/link key fields use the DB column names from lib/db/schema.ts. CQ golden answers are derived by reasoning each lib/query/templates.ts template against the graph below.

Feature.label: User Authentication
Feature.description: How a customer proves who they are before transacting.

Feature.label: Payment Processing
Feature.description: How a customer pays a merchant once authenticated.

Requirement.label: REQ-1
Requirement.text: Users authenticate with email and password.

Requirement.label: REQ-2
Requirement.text: Users complete a payment with a saved card.

Requirement.label: REQ-3
Requirement.text: Users reset their password via an emailed link.

Requirement.label: REQ-4
Requirement.text: The login endpoint sustains 10,000 requests/second at p99 < 200 ms.

Service.label: auth-service
Service.description: Handles sign-in and password reset for Helios.

Service.label: token-service
Service.description: Infrastructure service that mints and verifies session tokens.

Service.label: payment-service
Service.note: Deliberately absent from HLD/LLD so noDesignDoc=true.

Datastore.label: user-db
Datastore.purpose: System of record for user accounts/credentials.

Decision.label: ADR-001
Decision.rationale: JWT lets the auth-service and downstream services validate a session statelessly, avoiding a round trip to a central session store on every request.

Test.label: T1
Test.description: login integration test: signs in with email and password and asserts a session is returned.

Test.label: T2
Test.description: payment flow test: completes a payment with a saved card and asserts the charge succeeds.

LoadTestResult.label: login load test
LoadTestResult.scenario: Sustained load test of the Helios login endpoint (POST /login).

Q1.question: Which requirements have no verifying test?
Q1.derivation: VERIFIES edges exist only from T1->REQ-1 and T2->REQ-2. REQ-3 and REQ-4 have no incoming VERIFIES edge. REQ-4 is only VALIDATES'd by the load test, which is not a VERIFIES test. So the anti-join returns {REQ-3, REQ-4}.

Q2.question: Which services have no design doc / no load test?
Q2.derivation: noDesignDoc = no MENTIONS edge into the service from a chunk whose document is HLD/LLD/ARD. auth-service and token-service both appear verbatim in HLD.txt and LLD-auth.txt -> false; payment-service appears in no HLD/LLD -> true. noLoadTest = no VALIDATES edge from a LoadTestResult to any requirement the service IMPLEMENTS. auth-service IMPLEMENTS REQ-4 which the login load test VALIDATES -> false; token-service IMPLEMENTS nothing -> true; payment-service IMPLEMENTS REQ-2 (not load-tested) -> true.

Q3.question: What depends on token-service / what breaks if it changes?
Q3.derivation: Incoming DEPENDS_ON, transitive. auth-service -> token-service (direct dependent). payment-service -> auth-service -> token-service (transitive dependent). So the blast radius of token-service is {auth-service, payment-service}.

Q4.question: Show the PRD->LLD->impl->load-test chain for Feature User Authentication.
Q4.derivation: requirements = those that SPECIFY F1 = {REQ-1, REQ-3, REQ-4}. services = those that IMPLEMENT any of those reqs = auth-service (implements REQ-1/3/4). tests = VERIFIES into those reqs = T1 (verifies REQ-1). loadTestResults = VALIDATES into those reqs = login load test (validates REQ-4). REQ-2/T2/payment-service belong to F2 and are excluded.

Q5.question: What datastore does auth-service use?
Q5.derivation: USES edges from auth-service: auth-service -> user-db. So the answer is {user-db}.

Q6.question: Did the load test meet the PRD target for REQ-4?
Q6.derivation: The login load test VALIDATES REQ-4. Observed 8,000 req/s < target 10,000 req/s, and observed p99 250 ms > target 200 ms, so passed=false -> the target was MISSED.

Q7.question: What decisions affected auth-service, and why?

answer.rationale: JWT lets auth-service and downstream services validate a session statelessly, avoiding a central-session-store round trip per request, keeping the hot sign-in path fast under the 10,000 req/s target.

Q7.derivation: AFFECTS edges into auth-service: ADR-001 -> auth-service. So the answer is ADR-001 (JWT) with its rationale.

Q8.question: Who owns auth-service?
Q8.derivation: OWNS edges into auth-service: Alice Chen -> auth-service. So the owner is Alice Chen (Staff Engineer).

Q9.question: If Feature User Authentication changes, what is the full blast radius?
Q9.derivation: Impacted requirements = SPECIFIES F1 = {REQ-1, REQ-3, REQ-4}. Implementing services = auth-service. Transitive incoming DEPENDS_ON dependents of auth-service = payment-service (payment-service -> auth-service). So services = {auth-service, payment-service}. Verification artifacts on the impacted requirements: VERIFIES tests = T1 (REQ-1); VALIDATES loadtests = login load test (REQ-4). T2 verifies REQ-2 (F2) and is NOT included.

Q10.question: How does payment-service transitively depend on token-service?
Q10.derivation: Outgoing DEPENDS_ON from payment-service: payment-service -> auth-service -> token-service, giving the ordered path [payment-service, auth-service, token-service]. The reverse query (token-service depends on payment-service) has no outgoing DEPENDS_ON path -> empty.
